rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE L. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/26/24. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition was just e-filed on 5/9/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) As still no courtesy copies have been received by the assigned examiner, the amended petition and proof of service have not been reviewed in detail yet. Please provide courtesy copies of the amended petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/17/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLEN J. CHUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/17/2024 to be heard by the judicial officer who has been hearing this matter, Also, the following issues to be cleared two weeks before the hearing: (1) Notice of Hearing to Erica Chung should meet the 15-day requirement and be directly given to her in addition to her counsel. CRC 7.51(a)(b). Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance must be given. LRSF. 14.1.C.6). (2) Need information on how the funds advanced from Executor and Erica Chung (Receipt Schedule Page 42) are considered donation/contribution to the estate as no corresponding reimbursement has been made. (3) Disbursement to Silke Finnis $5,000 for correction of erroneous deposit (Disbursement Schedule page 106). What deposit? Nothing in the receipt dated 6/22/17 (4) Caregiving expenses paid for March to April in 2016, totaling $4,650 (Disbursement Schedule page 105) (5) Clarification if there has been no separate compensation in the ancillary proceeding in WA as this estate paid $4.394.76 in total. (Disbursement scheduled page 106) (6) Note: It looks like fees paid to professionals for preparation of the court accounting and taxes. The amount may be deducted from the statutory fee. LR14.51 (Disbursement Schedule page 107) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YESENIA SOSA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] This issue was raised previously in these proceedings. The time to administer the estate will not be extended due to procedural defects. Please provide a courtesy copy of the complete notice to the assigned examiner by 6/14/24. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Unfiled papers are recycled. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ANTHONY GEORGE CHOU-ROJAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although a fiduciary is required to sign and verify all of her own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment to a petition (other than her attorney's fee declaration), the financial statement ending 12/31/23 did not exactly verify the assets on hand that were transferred to the successor guardian on 11/16/23 (only ending statements are required for interim accountings), and the attorney time sheets did not set forth costs in detail (including the date, payee, and full description of each disbursement). (Pleadings addressing defects should stand on their own and not "respond" to issues posted by an examiner.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIE ANDREWS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative [DE-147] to be filed. (2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES DEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/26/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The I&A showed the estate at $925,000, not $950,000. (3) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN CONFINED in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (4) Attorney costs to be reimbursed must be itemized, showing the date incurred, payee, description, and each amount. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 614/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
